metadata.title: SYNGO_CC_4938
metadata.comment:
Rationale: Dp71 is a short product of Dystrophin (gene symbol DMD).

Figure 3 and 5: HA-Dp71 colocalises with Gephyrin (GPHN), an inhibitory postsynapse marker, in the hippocampal dentate gyrus (DG) granular layer of the transgenic (TG) mouse brain section (Fig. 3) and cultured hippocampal neurons (Fig. 5). Authors write: "Taken together, HA-Dp71 was exclusively localized to inhibitory postsynapses in the cultured hippocampal neurons."

WT vs HA-Dp71 control for HA-antibody specificity in Figure 2.

Supplementary Figure 6a: "In agreement with all above results, HA-Dp71 puncta were adjacent to GABAergic presynaptic terminals labelled by GAD67"

The gephyrin staining had cross reactivity with the nucleus in Figures 3 and 4, on which authors noted: "Arrows indicate strong cross-reactions against nuclear component revealed by anti-Gephyrin antibody which should be ignored". This brings the specificity of the gephyrin antibody into question. However, taken together with the presented co-localization with VGAT (Fig. 5c) and GAD67 (Fig. S6a), localization of Dp71 (DMD) to the postsynapse is likely.. Experimental description: From M&M:
Transgenic mousemodel: "Briefly, the crRNA guiding sequence for the intron 62 of dystrophin (Dmd) was designed as follows: 5'-GTGTTCCCTCATGGTTGTAA-3'. (...) For generating HA-tag-insertion mice, the recombinant Cas9 protein (50 ng/mL, New England Biolabs, MA, USA), crRNA (1.22 mol/μL), tracrRNA (1.22 mol/μL), and single strand oligonucleotide (100 ng/μL: 5'-GTCCGCCCCTCACTGCCTGTGAAACCCTTACAACCATGTACCCATACGATGTTCCAGATTACGCTATGAGGGAACACCTCAAAGGGTAAGTGGAT-3') were coinjected into the cytoplasm of pronuclear stage eggs from B6D2F1 mice."

Cultured neurons from transgenic mouse model: "Cultured hippocampal neurons and cultured non-neuronal glial cells were prepared from homozygous HA-Dp71-transgenic mouse embryos (Embryonic day-18) or postnatal day-0 pups as described previously [33]."

Antibodies:
HA-tag, C29F4, #3724, Rabbit, Cell Signaling Technology 2000(WB), 800(IF), Dp71 was tagged with HA, authors note: "it is impossible to distinguish expressions of different Dp71 isoforms immunohistochemically by tag detection in the transgenic mouse tissues, because the HA-tag was inserted at the N-terminus of Dp71 sequence. Thus, we herein characterized spatial expressions of Pan-Dp71 in the adult mouse brains and primary cultured cells."
